abstract.text: Manage macOS virtual machines from the terminal with the
CLI.

inlineContent.text: CLI creates, starts, stops, clones, and configures macOS
virtual machines on Apple Silicon. It uses the same
library as the GUI app and Kubernetes operator, so behavior is
CLI requires an Apple Silicon Mac running
macOS 14.0 or later. Apple Silicon supports a maximum of 2

abstract.text: Create and manage macOS virtual machines on Apple Silicon.
Runtime metadata for a virtual machine.
Run ML training and inference on Apple Silicon GPUs inside Spooktacular virtual machines.
The hardware specification for a virtual machine.
Manage macOS virtual machines as Kubernetes resources with custom CRDs and an operator.
The minimum number of CPU cores required for a macOS VM
to boot without freezing.
Connect to Spooktacular VMs via VNC for remote development, QA testing, and design review.
Run Spooktacular on AWS EC2 Mac dedicated hosts to double your macOS CI capacity.
The strategy for executing a user-data script on a VM.
Manages disk-level snapshots for virtual machine bundles.
Run self-hosted macOS GitHub Actions runners in Spooktacular VMs with ephemeral isolation and automatic scaling.
Resolves the IP address of a running VM by its MAC address.
A virtual machine bundle stored on disk.
Manages PID files for tracking running virtual machines.
Run two isolated OpenClaw AI agent instances on a single Mac — each with its own workspace, channels, and API keys.
Manages the local cache of VM images.
Creates copy-on-write clones of virtual machine bundles.
Create your first macOS virtual machine in minutes.
The network configuration mode for a virtual machine.
Understand what version numbers mean and what to expect from each release.
Set up a BlueBubbles iMessage server in a macOS VM — a dramatically simpler alternative to VMware on Windows.
A directory shared between the host and the virtual machine.
